response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, extensive experience, and excellent software craftsmanship practices, this role requires deep current expertise in C++ systems programming, industrial automation, and embedded systems—none of which match his recent career trajectory. His C++ experience dates to 2014–2015 (National Instruments), and his last 5+ years have focused on web services, full-stack development, and AI infrastructure using Node.js and TypeScript. The role's requirements (networking, multi-threading, MQTT, IEC 62443, industrial protocols) and domain focus (automation machinery, on-premises/cloud industrial solutions) are substantially misaligned with his current skill set and professional direction.
strengths: 19+ years of software engineering experience exceeds the 8–12 year requirement; BS in Computer Science from ETH Zürich meets educational criteria
Demonstrated mastery of software craftsmanship, testing, and quality mindset through TDD adoption, CI/CD practices, and creation of testing libraries (fluent-asserts, trial)
Strong ownership and cross-functional collaboration across diverse technical domains; experience mentoring and teaching others through workshops and public talks
concerns: C++ expertise is stale (last significant C++ work in 2014–2015 at National Instruments); primary focus for past 5+ years has been web technologies (Node.js, TypeScript, React, fullstack)
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT, networking, multi-threading, or systems-level concurrent programming—core requirements for this role
Lacks evidence of security expertise in industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), message brokers, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA) that the role requires

key_factors.factor: C++ and systems programming expertise currency
Industrial automation and IoT domain background
Software engineering fundamentals and quality mindset
